# Structured Table Project

Structured Table Language (STL) is a lightweight, human-readable syntax designed to define complex, responsive tables easily. It solves the problem of writing tedious HTML or Markdown tables, particularly when dealing with rowspans, colspans, custom styling, or interactive elements like buttons.

## Syntax Basics

### Basic Table
```stl
#table
cols: 3

[header]
First Name | Last Name | Age

[body]
John | Doe | 30
Jane | Smith | 25
```

### Spanning (Rowspan / Colspan)
Use `<` to span columns (colspan) leftwards.
Use `^` to span rows (rowspan) upwards.
```stl
#table
cols: 3
[body]
Header 1 | Header 2 | Header 3
Span 2 Cols | < | Normal
Normal | Span 2 Rows | Normal
Normal | ^ | Normal
```

### Alignment
Use `{align="left|center|right"}` at the start of a cell.
```stl
Name | {align="right"} $10.00
```

### Custom Classes
Use `{class="my-class"}` at the start of a cell.
```stl
{class="text-red-500 font-bold"} Out of Stock
```

### Line Breaks
Use `[br]` for inline line breaks within a cell.
```stl
123 Main St[br]Suite 400
```

### Buttons and Actions
Use `{type="button" action="actionName" data="optionalData"}`. Renderers will emit `st-action` events.
```stl
{type="button" action="delete" data="123"} Delete
```

### Links
Use `{type="link" href="https://example.com"}`
```stl
{type="link" href="/about" newTab="true"} About Us
```

## Renderers

### React
```bash
npx stl-cli add react
```
Usage:
```tsx
import * as STLReact from './components/react'
import { STL } from 'structured-table'

const tableData = STL.parse(stlString)
<STLReact.Table data={tableData} className="simple" />
```

### Vue
```bash
npx stl-cli add vue
```
Usage:
```vue
<script setup>
import { STLTable } from './components/vue'
import { STL } from 'structured-table'
const tableData = STL.parse(stlString)
</script>
<template>
  <STLTable :data="tableData" class="simple" />
</template>
```

### Svelte
```bash
npx stl-cli add svelte
```
Usage:
```svelte
<script>
  import Table from './components/svelte/Table.svelte'
  import { STL } from 'structured-table'
  const tableData = STL.parse(stlString)
</script>
<Table data={tableData} class="simple" />
```

## Sanity Studio Integration
```bash
npm install sanity-plugin-stl-table
```
Import `stlTableBlock` schema definition.
```typescript
import { stlTableBlock } from 'sanity-plugin-stl-table'
```
*Note: We recommend parsing `stlString` at runtime on the frontend rather than querying the deprecated `stlParsed` field.*
